Shoulder Range of Motion Smartform Use

Cameron Stahl

March 06, 2026

Basics of this Data

Rows Analyzed

This data includes documentation from 503 clinicians practicing in 71 clinics. Data is from a recorded date of 2025-10-13 through 2026-02-23, and covers all of the shoulder range of motion options listed below.

Row Use

Active and Passive Range of Motion

Within Functional Limits vs Within Normal Limits

Overall “Within Normal Limits” has seen much more use than “Within Functional Limits”. While both were used, this may prompt a decision from Rehab leadership on whether keeping both is necessary or if we can reduce visual clutter and simplify documentation as they have largely overlapping meaning.

Comments for WFL and WNL

This plot combines WFL and WNL documentation as well as comments for both. Commenting for WFL and WNL was extremely limited. The ability to remove commenting options would likely help reduce visual clutter without significant impact on documentation ability.

Clinician ROM Documentation Trends Over Time

This plot trends clinician use of the active and passive number entry boxes over two week intervals. The “Abandoned” group holds clinicians who have documentation during the first month but who haven’t documented at all in the last month of data.

Additional Details Row: Numeric ROM Documentation

ROM Documentation Method by Department

Appendix

Additional Details: Numeric ROM Match Reference

Additional Details entries containing numeric ROM values. Only the matched text snippet (with 20 characters of surrounding context) is shown, not the full entry. Note: short keywords (ER, IR) may occasionally match word endings.
